Getting Here

Travel & stay

We've put together a couple of room block options below. Both are right in the heart of the city, equidistant from the welcome party and wedding venue, but feel free to stay wherever works best for you.

The Carpenter Hotel
400 Josephine St, Austin, TX 78704
Tucked under pecan trees at the edge of Zilker Park, this boutique hotel is full of character. Madeline and Jake will be staying here, too. Our block here is limited, so we encourage booking early.
Book Your Room →
Embassy Suites South Congress
300 S Congress Ave, Austin, TX 78704
A more traditional hotel experience with spacious suites at a different price point. It's also just steps from the Congress Avenue Bridge, home to the famous colony of Mexican free-tailed bats.
Book Your Room →
Flying In? Austin-Bergstrom International Airport (AUS) is your closest airport. Rideshares are widely available. Book early because Austin fills up fast on spring weekends!

While You're Here

Things to do

Austin has so much to offer, and we hope you'll take some time to explore beyond the wedding weekend. Here are a few of our favorites.

Outdoors
Lady Bird Lake Trail
The Hike-and-Bike Trail takes you on a beautiful loop around the lake. You can pick your distance with 3, 4, 5-mile, or longer loops. Great for a walk or jog!
Outdoors
Butler Pitch and Putt
A classic, low-key nine-hole course along the trail. Perfect for a relaxed afternoon.
Outdoors
Barton Springs Pool
A spring-fed pool that stays cool year-round, right in the heart of Zilker Park. A must for a sunny Austin afternoon.
Fun
Peter Pan Mini Golf
A quirky, retro mini golf course that's been an Austin staple for decades. Great for a group outing.
Shopping
South Congress
Boutiques, vintage shops, and people-watching along one of Austin's most iconic streets.
Food
Terry Black's Barbecue
Central Texas barbecue done right. Come hungry! The lines move fast and the brisket is worth it.
Food
The Green Mesquite
Down-home Texas barbecue and comfort food in a laid-back, no-frills spot. Check the schedule because there's often live music in the backyard.
Drinks
JuiceLand
Austin's favorite juice and smoothie spot.
Drinks
Barton Springs Saloon
A neighborhood dive with a great patio.

Questions

FAQ

When should I RSVP by? +
Please RSVP by February 1, 2027 so we can get final counts to our vendors. If something comes up after that, reach out to us directly by text or at mad.jake.wedding@gmail.com.
What's the weather like in Austin in May? +
May in Austin is warm and gorgeous. Expect highs around 85°F during the day and comfortable evenings around 65–70°F. The ceremony and reception are outdoors, so light layers for the evening aren't a bad idea. And yes, there's always a small chance of a Texas spring shower!
What's the dress code? +
Bright and bold! We're celebrating in full color. Wear your most vibrant outfit and favorite patterns. Think florals, prints, and anything that makes you feel festive.
Can I bring a plus one? +
Please check your invitation to see if a plus one is included. If you're not sure, feel free to reach out to us directly.
I have a dietary restriction. Will there be options for me? +
Absolutely! Please note any dietary restrictions or allergies in your RSVP and we'll make sure you're taken care of. When in doubt, reach out to us directly.
What time should I arrive at the ceremony? +
Please plan to arrive by 5:30 PM. The ceremony will begin promptly at 6:00 PM. Give yourself a little extra time for traffic and a short walk from the parking lot.
Is there parking at the Wildflower Center? +
Yes! Free parking is available on site at the Lady Bird Johnson Wildflower Center. Rideshares are also a great option if you plan to celebrate with a drink or two.
Are you changing your last names? +
Nope, not planning on it! Same names, just officially a team now.

Madeline & Jake

Our story

December 21, 2021
Our first date
We met on Hinge and had our first date on the Winter Solstice, a rainy Tuesday at a local brewery in the Haight-Ashbury neighborhood.
April 2022
First trip to Yosemite
Our first solo trip together was a springtime visit to Yosemite where we hiked to the top of Yosemite Falls and almost got stuck in a surprise snowstorm.
August 2022
First international trip
Our relationship went international. We visited Oaxaca and Puerto Escondido in Mexico and experienced the local cuisine, mezcal, art, and OTC antibiotics.
July 2023
Marathoned together
In 2023, we trained together and ran the Oakland half marathon and the San Francisco full marathon, finishing at the same time. Oh the hills!
November 2023
Barcelona with family
We met up with Jake's mom, Linda in Barcelona and spent the week visiting beautiful cathedrals and exploring the Gothic Quarter.
May 2024
Moved in together
We each gave up our bachelor pad 1-bedroom apartments and moved in together, joining our lives more fully and learning a lot about love in the process.
December 2024
Went out west
We took a road trip with Madeline's family to Big Bend National Park in West Texas and crossed the border into Boquillas del Carmen on "well-trained" donkeys.
May 2025
Tried out van life
We rented a van in Boise and road-tripped through Idaho, Montana, and Wyoming, hitting all the national parks, roadside curiosities, and wildlife along the way. Bonus: spending time with family in Sun Valley.
November 15, 2025
We got engaged!
Jake planned an amazing and elaborate engagement full of whimsical surprises, and Madeline got the ring of her dreams.
And now we're getting married. May 1, 2027 · Austin, Texas
